ground_truth.title: 6 Best Personal Trainer Certifications
ground_truth.author: Hannah Daugherty
ground_truth.main_content:
The best personal trainer certifications are highly recognized within the fitness industry and allow you to design training programs, guide clients through workouts, and build a fitness business – whether in a gym, online, or in your own studio. The quality of your certification can shape not only your knowledge but also your job prospects, salary, and credibility.

Choosing the proper certification is crucial for success, so we’ve ranked six top certified personal training programs based on what matters most to aspiring trainers: getting certified quickly, keeping costs reasonable, and actually landing a job. Our research-driven scoring system evaluates each certification across six practical factors that directly impact your career success – from how fast you can start working to the salary you can expect to earn.

1. ISSA Certified Personal Trainer
Best For Guaranteed Job Placement (19/24 points)
Pros:
- Only certification with a money-back guarantee if a job is not found within 6 months of certification
- Highest pass rate at 90%
- Unlimited exam time and free retakes
- Comprehensive study materials (videos, audio lectures, workbooks)
- 100% online, self-paced with no deadlines
- Strong international recognition
- Emphasis on practical skills and business acumen
- Must be at least 18 years old with a high school diploma or equivalent before testing
- Must hold a current CPR + AED certificate (complete with hands-on skills check) before testing
Cons:
- Most expensive certification option
- Shorter validity period (2 years vs 3 for some others)
- Unproctored exam may carry less weight with some employers
- Not ideal for advanced strength and conditioning focus
- Must pass the NCCPT Certified Personal Trainer NCCA exam as well as apply and interview with at least three gyms within 6 months of completing your certification course in order to qualify for money-back guarantee.
Summary:
The ISSA-CPT, from the International Sports Sciences Association, earned the highest score in our rankings at 19/24. Despite being the most expensive option, it’s the only certification offering a 6-month job placement guarantee—making it ideal for career changers who need income certainty.
Best For:
Career changers and beginners who value job security over cost, prefer flexible online learning, and want the confidence of a job guarantee—especially those planning to work internationally or online.
Key Stats:
- Time to Certify: 2-3 months, up to 6 months allowed (3 points)
- Cost: Starting at $868 (1 point – relatively expensive)
- Job Placement: No; however, it includes a 100% money-back guarantee if a job is not found within 6 months of certification. (5 points – only cert with this)
- Recognition: NCCA-accredited, accepted globally in 160+ countries (5 points)
- Program Rigor: 160 questions, online, unproctored, no time limit (3 points)
- Average Salary: ~$50,000/year (2 points)
- Pass Rate: 90% (highest among all certifications)
- Validity: 2 years, requires 20 CE hours for renewal

2. NASM Certified Personal Trainer
Best for Industry Recognition (18/24 points)
Pros:
- Strongest brand recognition in the industry
- Best digital learning platform with video-heavy content
- Systematic approach to assessments and corrective exercise
- Highly respected by all gym types and private clients
- Self-paced online study with excellent resources
- Must be at least 18 years old with a high school diploma or equivalent before testing
- Must hold a current CPR + AED certificate (complete with hands-on skills check) before testing
Cons:
- No job placement support despite premium pricing
- One of the toughest exams requiring deep preparation
- Higher cost than most competitors
- Strict retake policy (escalating wait times: 1 week after first attempt, 30 days after second attempt, then 1 year after third attempt)
- Salary claims may be optimistic compared to industry averages
Summary:
The NASM-CPT, offered by the National Academy of Sports Medicine, scored 18/24 in our rankings, the second-highest score. It’s NCCA-accredited and often referred to as the gold standard in the industry. The NASM-CPT earns perfect marks for employer recognition despite its higher cost and lack of job placement support.
Best For:
Those who want the most recognized certification and can afford the higher upfront cost, especially if working in commercial gyms, private training, or online fitness where the NASM brand carries significant weight.
Key Stats:
- Time to Certify: 2-3 months (even sooner possible) (4 points)
- Cost: Starting at $799 (2 points – higher cost)
- Job Placement: No support offered (1 point)
- Recognition: NCCA-accredited, considered the gold standard by employers (5 points – top score)
- Program Rigor: 120 questions, 2 hours, proctored exam (3 points)
- Average Salary: ~$126,880/year (per NASM: $61/hour) (3 points)
- Pass Rate: 65%
- Validity: 2 years, requires 2.0 CEUs for renewal

3. NCSF Certified Personal Trainer
Best for Budget-Friendly Learning (15/24 points)
Pros:
- One of the most affordable among major certifications
- Highest pass rate at 79%
- Accepted by all major gym chains
- International recognition in 160+ countries
- Digital Learning Portal with videos, quizzes, and flashcards
- Strong focus on functional anatomy and evidence-based practice
Cons:
- No job placement assistance
- Less brand recognition than NASM or ACE
- Limited advanced specialization options
- Must be at least 18 years old with a high school diploma or equivalent before testing
- Must hold a current CPR + AED certificate (complete with hands-on skills check) before testing
Summary:
The NCSF-CPT, from the National Council on Strength & Fitness, scored 15/24 points. It’s the most affordable NCCA-accredited certification and offers excellent value for those on a tight budget who still want a respected credential.
Best For:
Budget-conscious beginners who want an NCCA-accredited certification without breaking the bank, especially those planning to work in commercial gyms where NCSF is widely accepted.
Key Stats:
- Time to Certify: 2-3 months (4 points)
- Cost: Starting at $499 (5 points – most affordable NCCA option)
- Job Placement: No support (1 point)
- Recognition: NCCA-accredited, accepted by major gym chains (4 points)
- Program Rigor: 150 questions, 3 hours, proctored exam (3 points)
- Average Salary: ~$45,000/year (2 points)
- Pass Rate: 79%
- Validity: 2 years, requires 2.0 CEUs for renewal

4. National Personal Trainer Institute (NPTI)
Best for Hands-On Learning (14/24 points)
Pros:
- Only program offering live, in-person training
- Department of Education recognized (not just NCCA)
- Earn both a diploma and certification
- Small class sizes with personalized attention
- Strong focus on practical, hands-on skills
- Must be at least 18 years old with a high school diploma or equivalent before testing
- Must hold a current CPR + AED certificate (complete with hands-on skills check) before testing
Cons:
- Most expensive option overall
- Requires physical attendance (not fully online)
- Limited locations nationwide
- Longer time commitment (500 hours)
- Less flexible for working students
Summary:
NPTI scored 14/24 points and stands out as the only program offering comprehensive hands-on training. While it’s the most expensive and least flexible option, it provides the most thorough practical education.
Best For:
Those who learn best through hands-on instruction and can commit to in-person training, especially career changers who want maximum practical experience before entering the field.
Key Stats:
- Time to Certify: 6-12 months (1 point – longest duration)
- Cost: $7,000-$12,000 (1 point – most expensive)
- Job Placement: Strong network and assistance (4 points)
- Recognition: Dept of Education recognized + NCCA (5 points)
- Program Rigor: 500 hours hands-on + written exam (5 points)
- Average Salary: ~$52,000/year (3 points)
- Pass Rate: 85%
- Validity: 2 years, requires 2.0 CEUs for renewal

5. Fitness Mentors Certified Personal Trainer
Best for Fast Certification (13/24 points)
Pros:
- Fastest certification option (as little as 4 weeks)
- Lowest cost among all options
- Excellent customer support
- High exam pass rate
- 100% online, self-paced
- Must be at least 18 years old with a high school diploma or equivalent before testing
- Must hold a current CPR + AED certificate (complete with hands-on skills check) before testing
Cons:
- Not NCCA-accredited (NBFE-accredited instead)
- Less recognized by major gym chains
- Limited advanced education options
- Less comprehensive than other programs
- May not satisfy all employer requirements
Summary:
Fitness Mentors scored 13/24 points and is ideal for those who need to get certified quickly and affordably. While not NCCA-accredited, it’s a legitimate option for trainers working independently or in smaller facilities.
Best For:
Those who need to start working ASAP with minimal investment, particularly online trainers or those working in smaller, independent gyms that don’t require NCCA accreditation.
Key Stats:
- Time to Certify: 4-8 weeks (5 points – fastest)
- Cost: Starting at $397 (5 points – lowest cost)
- Job Placement: Limited support (2 points)
- Recognition: NBFE-accredited, not NCCA (2 points)
- Program Rigor: 100 questions, online, unproctored (2 points)
- Average Salary: ~$42,000/year (1 point)
- Pass Rate: 88%
- Validity: 2 years, requires 20 CE hours for renewal

6. NSCA Certified Personal Trainer
Best for Strength & Conditioning Focus (12/24 points)
Pros:
- Gold standard for strength and conditioning
- Highly respected in athletic and clinical settings
- Strong scientific foundation
- Excellent for career advancement
- Must be at least 18 years old with a high school diploma or equivalent before testing
- Must hold a current CPR + AED certificate (complete with hands-on skills check) before testing
Cons:
- Most difficult exam
- Highest failure rate
- Expensive
- Not ideal for general population training
- Requires college-level science knowledge
Summary:
The NSCA-CPT scored 12/24 points and is considered the premier certification for strength and conditioning specialists. It’s the most challenging option but carries immense prestige in athletic and clinical settings.
Best For:
Those with a strong science background who want to work with athletes or in clinical settings, or trainers looking to specialize in strength and conditioning.
Key Stats:
- Time to Certify: 3-6 months (3 points)
- Cost: Starting at $475 (4 points)
- Job Placement: No support (1 point)
- Recognition: NCCA-accredited, gold standard for S&C (5 points)
- Program Rigor: 150 questions, 3 hours, very difficult (2 points)
- Average Salary: ~$55,000/year (3 points)
- Pass Rate: 55% (lowest)
- Validity: 3 years, requires 6.0 CEUs for renewal
with: The best personal trainer certifications are highly recognized within the fitness industry and allow you to design training programs, guide clients through workouts, and build a fitness business – whether in a gym, online, or in your own studio.

The ISSA-CPT, from the International Sports Sciences Association, earned the highest score in our rankings at 19/24. Despite being the most expensive option, it’s the only certification offering a 6-month job placement guarantee—making it ideal for career changers who need income certainty.

The NASM-CPT, offered by the National Academy of Sports Medicine, scored 18/24 in our rankings, the second-highest score. It’s NCCA-accredited and often referred to as the gold standard in the industry.
without: 87,455 People have used this article to select a personal trainer certification
